_readme.naming-note: Brand term for this object is 'token' (the postcard/print sold with the story; user-facing copy says 'token'). The JSON field is still named `talisman` in code — left unchanged on purpose.
_readme.what: Single source of truth for the Eleanora Journal. journal.html (the wall), the homepage teaser, and every entry's token-close + read-next all render from this file. Add a story by appending one object to `entries` — never hand-edit the wall or the teaser.
_readme.buckets: Every entry has one `bucket`, mapped straight off the Content Wheel tracker's `purpose` field: 'the-long-way' (lessons — the hard-won takeaways) · 'the-archive' (stories — personal narrative + travel memoir; a trip still pairs to a Collection via exits) · 'a-place-to-park' (thought-provokers — food for thought, 'park that thought') · 'curio' (The Curio Cabinet — the odd, personal, off-the-wheel wonderings). Umbrella / soul line for the whole Journal is 'the way I see it'. The wall filter buttons are built from these; a filter auto-hides until its bucket has at least one entry, so an empty bucket never shows an empty wall.
_readme.the-token-rule: The feature image IS the token — the print sold at the close of the entry. The code field is `talisman` (unchanged): `talisman.img` is what shows on the wall card AND the buy block. `talisman.buyUrl` stays null until GoDaddy Payments (pay.corraoelena.com) is live; until then the buy button shows 'Prints coming soon' (same mock as collections.json). Never fake a live price/checkout.
_readme.the-exits: `exits.collection` = a slug in collections.json (Travels entries → 'see the whole trip'). `exits.manual` true = Way-I-See-It entries end on the hook toward the paid manual. `soft` true (People) = a gentle 'a token to remember them by', no hard sell.
_readme.read-next: Bottom of each entry shows 3 related, not-yet-visited entries (relatedness = shared `tags`, then same bucket). Visited slugs are tracked in the browser (localStorage).
_readme.proposed-tokens: Token image/name matches are PROPOSED (from token-photo-article-map.md) until Elena confirms on the live site. Working names are in [brackets]. See handoff notes for reconcile items.
_readme.how-to-add-an-entry:
1) Write the story page site/journal-.html from the template (story +
+ , body data-slug='journal-'). 2) Append an object here with matching slug. That's it — wall, teaser, token-close, exits, and read-next all wire automatically.

entries.title: The Girl Behind the Camera (but also in front)
entries.excerpt: I'll start with a confession. You've seen me somewhere before. You just can't place it. The whole story, from the girl who wanted to be someone to the one still searching for home.

talisman.alt: Eleanora in black and white, a feather at her shoulder and soft smoke curling
talisman.name: [working title — the girl you can't place]
talisman.line: What if this picture reminded you, every day, that you've always been someone worth becoming? If you believed it could, would it?

entries.title: The Good Girl (who was already gone)
entries.excerpt: I made my Confirmation the year I decided to leave. The story of the good girl who was already gone, and the map she built out of her own life.

talisman.alt: Eleanora in an open fur coat and lace shorts, black and white
talisman.name: The Canvas
talisman.line: Make my dreams come true. Put a little of my art on your wall.

entries.title: Drowning, but in Croatia
entries.excerpt: I quit my job to come here, looking for a sign. And boy, did I find one — the first thing this country said to me, scrawled across a wall.

talisman.alt: A small green island floating in the calm blue sea off Dubrovnik
talisman.name: Island at Sea
talisman.line: What if this picture reminded you, every day, that the leap into open water was worth it? If you believed it could, would it?

entries.title: The Thing We Keep Running From
entries.excerpt: The world runs on two speeds. Routine, which it calls living. Complacency, which it calls comfort. I found the third thing in Italy.

talisman.alt: A yellow doorway with climbing flowers and a small red mailbox in Bari, Italy
talisman.name: The Flower Door
talisman.line: What if this picture reminded you, every day, to let your life bloom in its own hour? If you believed it could, would it?

entries.title: I Think I've Been Collecting Portals
entries.excerpt: Small confession. I have a thing for doors. Old ones. The heavy wooden kind with the paint peeling off. I have hundreds of them. What if I was never photographing doors at all — but little portals, asking each one the same quiet thing.

talisman.alt: An ancient weathered doorway in a narrow old street, paint peeling from the wood
talisman.name: [working title — the portal]
talisman.line: What if this picture reminded you, every day, that every old door is a question worth asking? If you believed it could, would it?
